name: West Sumbawa (Scar Reef, Super Suck, Yoyos) / 西松巴哇 / Sumbawa Barat
gps_location: Maluk / Sekongkang area, West Sumbawa, approx. -8.96, 116.74 (Maluk town); Scar Reef approx. -8.99, 116.70; Super Suck approx. -8.86, 116.74; Yoyos approx. -9.05, 116.74 [uncertain]
wave_type: Three premier reef breaks: Scar Reef (long left point/reef break, 150m walls with barrel sections), Super Suck (heavy hollow left tube, narrow barrel sucking off shallow reef, up to 150m), Yoyos / The Hook (right-hand reef break, long rippable walls). All reef bottom.
skill_level: Advanced to Pro. Super Suck and Scar Reef are expert-only. Yoyos accommodates intermediate to advanced.
wave_height_range: 4-10+ ft (head-high to triple overhead). Scar Reef needs 5ft+ SW swell to break properly; Yoyos consistent 6-8ft in June-July.
best_season: Dry season April-October; prime June-September with most consistent SW groundswells. Peak months June-July.
best_tide: Varies by spot: Scar Reef best at low tide (brutal but mechanical); Super Suck best mid to low, some sources suggest pushing to high tide to avoid shallow coral heads; Yoyos / The Hook surfable all tides but caution at lower tides on reef.
best_wind: Offshore SE to E trade winds; ENE for Yoyos. Easterly trades groom faces clean through dry season.
swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent during dry season; SW swells arrive weekly June-July)
wave_length_meters: Scar Reef up to 150m; Super Suck up to 150m; Yoyos long walls (100m+) [uncertain exact Yoyos length]
wave_speed_rating: fast (Super Suck and Scar Reef are fast, hollow, freight-train style); Yoyos medium-fast with rippable sections
takeoff_zone_difficulty: High. Super Suck has steep sucking takeoff over very shallow reef; Scar Reef heavy drops with shallow coral; Yoyos easier takeoff with channel access.
secondary_peaks: Yoyos has separate sections including The Hook (main right), The Wedge, and inside sections. Additional nearby breaks include Tropical Lefts, Tropical Rights, Playgrounds, providing options on different swell/wind combos.
peak_crowd_level: 2 (low to moderate; West Sumbawa noted for uncrowded world-class waves; can rise to 3 on prime swells when boat charters arrive)
access: Multi-leg overland: Bali (Padang Bai) ferry to Lembar Lombok (4-6 hrs), drive across Lombok to Kayangan port (~2 hrs), ferry to Poto Tano Sumbawa (1.5-2 hrs), drive to Maluk (~2 hrs). Total approx. 12-16 hrs door-to-door. Alternative: fly DPS->LOP (Lombok) then ferry/drive. Boat charter from Benoa Bali via Lembongan, Desert Point Lombok, then to West Sumbawa.
paddle_out_method: Scar Reef: paddle from beach or boat-in; Super Suck: typically boat-in from Maluk Bay or long paddle around the headland; Yoyos: paddle out via deep channel adjacent to The Hook peak from beach in front of Yoyos accommodation.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol and early morning preferred before onshore winds build; afternoon glass-off sessions possible during clean offshore periods.
boat_charter_required: Not strictly required if staying in Maluk/Sekongkang area (land access available to all three), but boat charter is the classic premium way to surf the region and is required for some outer reefs.
combo_trip_potential: Excellent - frequently combined with Lombok (Desert Point), Nusa Lembongan, and continuing east to Sumba/Rote on extended boat charters out of Bali.
hazards: Sharp shallow reef (primary), strong currents, remote location with limited rescue, board damage common, sharks reported in regional waters, sun exposure
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef, occasional jellyfish, sharks (rare encounters but reported), stonefish in shallow reef pools. Carry vinegar, reef-cut antiseptic, and reef booties recommended.
lineup_etiquette: Respect priority on premium waves; locals and guides at Scar Reef are friendly; foreign expat/charter surfers form bulk of lineup on big days - standard reef-break priority applies.
localism_intensity: low to medium (welcoming local crew at Scar Reef; respectful behavior expected, especially during boat charter season)
respect_protocol: Smile and greet locals, support local warungs and boat operators, do not snake waves, hire local boat captains for offshore access, respect village customs in Maluk/Sekongkang.
medical_nearby: Local clinic in Maluk reputedly experienced with reef-cut stitches (the joke is 'Scar Reef Stitches' as local specialty). Serious injuries require medevac to Bali (BIMC/Siloam Denpasar) via Lombok or charter flight.
rescue_jet_ski_available: No standing jet ski rescue. Some surf charters and resorts (e.g., Scar Reef Resort) may have boat support available [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesian general emergency 112; Maluk Puskesmas clinic [uncertain specific number]; insurance medevac line essential
accommodation_options: Scar Reef Resort (premium surf resort), Yoyos Surf Resort, Baha Baha Villas (Yoyo's Beach, yoga + surf), Maluk House (overlooking Super Suck), Balong-Balong Surf Bungalows, Jepun Sunset Bungalow (Sekongkang), Tropical Resort (formerly Nomad). Price range USD 30-150/night.
webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam confirmed; Surfline forecast pages available for Scar Reef and Supersuck [uncertain]
annual_competition_window: No major WSL CT/CS event; occasional regional/charter-based events; not a fixed competition venue [uncertain]
community_events: No regular weekly community events (Single Fin / Old Mans equivalent) - West Sumbawa is a low-key surf zone with no scheduled community calendar [uncertain]
